member.summary: Options for how to continue plist/patgen execution when the specified maximum number of failures have occurred.

member.name: F:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.ExecutionMaxFailBehavior.ExitPListOnFail
member.summary: The current burst is allowed to complete but the next burst is not executed

member.name: F:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.ExecutionMaxFailBehavior.AbortOnFail
member.summary: An attempt is made to abort patgen immediately. This is not compatible with KeepAlive plists.

member.name: F:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.ExecutionMaxFailBehavior.ContinueOnFail
member.summary: Plist execution continues (without capturing any additional failures).

member.name: F:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.ExecutionMaxFailBehavior.ReturnOnFail
member.summary: PList will evaluate fails at the pattern boundaries and return through the plist PostExec content.

member.name: T:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IFunctionalTest
member.summary: An interface for a functional test.

member.name: M:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IFunctionalTest.ValidateSettings
member.summary: Validates the functional test settings, including that: 1. The plist exists 2. The levels and timing test conditions exist (regardless of whether test conditions are automatically applied before executing the plist) 3. Any PatternTriggerMap exists and is valid for the plist 4. All edge counter pins exist, are in one of the plist domains, and have valid modes 5. All mask pins exist and are in one of the plist domains 6. Any start pattern information is valid?=.

member.summary: Executes the functional test and returns an object that contains results and provides access to capture data.

member.summary: Gets the overall number of failures per pin. Only failing pins will have entries.

member.name: M:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IResult.CheckAlarm
member.summary: This API is only for TOS3 to check and rethrow stored alarm during plist execution. It is to allow capture data return and mimic TOS4 behavior. Check for alarm. Throw if there is alarm. This is to allow functional test result to propagate back from c++ layer and read all necessary result before throwing alarm exception.

member.name: T:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPatternVectorContainer
member.summary: Class to add multiple pattern modify configuration inputs and execute read or write tasks based in that data.

member.name: M:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPatternVectorContainer.AddPinVectorRange(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.UInt64,System.UInt64)
member.summary: Creates and immediately validates an object that can read and write pattern pin vectors using an absolute address within the pattern and adds it to the container. Validation consists of verifying the pattern exists and contains the domain, the start address and vector count are valid, and the pins belong to the specified domain. The existing vector types are not checked. The returned object can be used to read or write data for the specified pins only.
member.param: The pins to read/modify; the domain is inferred from the pins, and all pins must be from the same domain.
The start address to read/modify within the pattern.
The number of vectors to read/modify.

member.name: M:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPatternVectorContainer.AddPinVectorRange(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.UInt64,System.UInt64,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates and immediately validates an object that can read and write pattern pin vectors using an absolute address within the pattern and adds it to the container. Validation consists of verifying the pattern exists and contains the domain, the start address and vector count are valid, and the pins belong to the specified domain. The existing vector types are not checked. The returned object can be used to read or write data for the specified pins only.
member.param: The pins to read/modify; the domain is inferred from the pins, and all pins must be from the same domain.
The start address to read/modify within the pattern.
The number of vectors to read/modify.
Indicates if channel link is used. FOR TOS3 ONLY.

member.summary: Creates and immediately validates an object that can read and write pattern pin vectors using an absolute address within the pattern and adds it to the container. The existing vector types are not checked. The returned object can be used to read or write data for the specified pins only.
member.param: The pins to read/modify; the domain is inferred from the pins, and all pins must be from the same domain.
The start address to read/modify within the pattern.
The number of vectors to read/modify.
Indicates if channel link is used. FOR TOS3 ONLY.
Indicates if fill data is used. FOR TOS3 ONLY.

The number of vectors to read/modify.

member.name: M:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPatternVectorContainer.AddConvertedMetadataVectorRange(System.String,System.String,System.UInt64,System.UInt64)
member.summary: Creates and immediately validates an object that can write pattern instruction vectors regardless of the current vector types in the range, using an absolute address within the pattern. The vectors will be converted to instruction vectors on write. This range cannot be used for reading (and will not be populated when as called). Validation consists of verifying the pattern exists and contains the domain and the start address and vector count are valid.
member.param: The start address to read/modify within the pattern.
The number of vectors to read/modify.

member.summary: Creates and immediately validates an object that can write pattern instruction vectors regardless of the current vector types in the range, using a label and offset within the pattern The vectors will be converted to instruction vectors on write. This range cannot be used for reading (and will not be populated when as called). Validation consists of verifying the pattern exists and contains the domain and the start address and vector count are valid.
member.param: With the offset, the start address to read/modify within the pattern.
The offset from the label to read/modify within the pattern.
The number of vectors to read/modify.

member.name: P:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPvcVectorRange.Ctv
member.summary: Gets or sets the CTV values for the specified pins. This will be populated on read. If modified, the CTV flags will be changed on write. This value is a single string, with one character for each vector in the range. A '1' character means capture this vector, a '0' character means do not capture this vector.

member.name: P:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPvcVectorRange.Mtv
member.summary: Gets or sets the MTV values for the specified pins. This will be populated on read. If modified, the MTV flags will be changed on write. This value is a single string, with one character for each vector in the range. A '1' character means mask all pins, a '0' character means mask no pins.

member.name: P:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPvcVectorRange.Lrpt
member.summary: Gets or sets the LRPT values for the specified pins. This will be populated on read. If modified, the LRPT values will be changed on write. This is a set integers, with one value per vector in the range.

member.name: T:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPlist
member.summary: Plist interface. Contains all the APIs to interact with plists.

member.name: M:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPlist.GetPatternsAndIndexes(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the pattern and nested plist names contained in the plist as well as their indexes.
member.remarks: From the hdmtOS: When getHWExecSequence is true, returns all pattern names for a Plist in order of execution from Hardware. Any nested plist content will be returned as flattened pattern names. plistItemType will be always "Pattern". The hardware execution sequence is the sequence currently in hardware. Changes made in software are not written to hardware until a PList is executed. When getHWExecSequence is false, returns all pattern and nested Plist names for a Plist in order described in .plist file in OTPL. Nested Plist will not get flattened. plistItemType will be returned as type "Pattern" for patterns and "PList" for nested plist.\n The latest flattened execution sequence can be obtained by setting getHWExecSequence=false, and making repeated calls to GetPatternsAndIndexesInPlist for each "PList" type found in the resulting list.
member.param: Name of the plist to retrieve the patterns and nested plists from.
If get the HW execution sequence of patterns.
member.returns: A list of PlistContent type with all pattern and nested plists.

member.name: M:Prime.PlatformService.Internal.IPlist.UnloadPlist(System.String)

member.name: T:Prime.PlatformService.PlistElementExecutionType
member.summary: The execution type for a plist or pattern element.

member.name: F:Prime.PlatformService.PlistElementExecutionType.PreExec
member.summary: The plist or pattern is executed before any main patterns or referenced plists. This is the equivalent of the PreExecPat keyword for patterns and the PreExecRefPList for referenced plists.

member.name: F:Prime.PlatformService.PlistElementExecutionType.Main
member.summary: The plist or pattern is executed as part of the main sequence. This is the equivalent of the Pat or RefPList keywords

member.name: F:Prime.PlatformService.PlistElementExecutionType.PostExec
member.summary: The plist or pattern is executed after any main patterns or referenced plists. This is the equivalent of the PostExecPat keyword for patterns and the PostExecRefPList keyword for referenced plists.
